goal: Land the `find_references` MCP tool on `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs`, wire it into the WO-0047 G1 orchestrator + WO-0048 G1 fusion pipeline via 4 production-shape `G1Source` impls (`KgG1Source` for tree-sitter+KG, `SerenaG1Source` for `Arc<dyn SerenaHoverClient>`, `AstGrepG1Source` and `DiagnosticsG1Source` as production-shape stubs that report `G1ToolStatus::Unavailable` until their subprocess channels light up in a follow-up WO), and group the fused entries by `usage_type` field (`call` / `import` / `type-annotation`) per master-plan §3.2 row 3.
plan_summary: Add `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g1_sources.rs` (NEW module) with the 4 `G1Source` impls; extend `McpServer` with an optional `Arc<G1SourceFactory>` slot that constructs a fresh `Vec<Box<dyn G1Source + Send + Sync + 'static>>` per request (factory pattern keeps the server `Clone` and avoids `execute_g1`'s consume-by-move signature problem); register a `find_references` route on `handle_tools_call` that calls `execute_g1` + `fuse_g1` and groups the resulting `G1FusedEntry`s by their `fields["usage_type"]` (string) — emit `_meta.references_by_usage = { call: [...], import: [...], type-annotation: [...] }`. Add per-source children spans `ucil.group.structural.source` on `run_g1_source` (closes the WO-0047 lessons line 296 deferral on per-source instrumentation; the parent `ucil.group.structural` span on `execute_g1` already landed in WO-0047). Refactor the `.expect(...)` at `executor.rs:1057` to a structurally-enforced `let-else` per WO-0047 lessons line 308. Frozen acceptance test `pub async fn test_find_references_tool()` lives at MODULE ROOT of `server.rs` per DEC-0007 and exercises the full handler pipeline against 4 local TestG1Source impls (per DEC-0008 — UCIL-owned trait, local impls in tests are NOT critical-dep mocks). Lib.rs preamble paragraph for WO-0048 gets a NEW trailing sentence appended naming F05's deliverable; re-exports for every new `pub` symbol land in this WO (carry the cumulative-debt-avoidance discipline from WO-0042 / 0043 / 0044 / 0045 / 0046 / 0047 / 0048 — now 7 consecutive).
scope_in:
Create `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g1_sources.rs` (NEW file). Module-level `//!` doc cites master-plan §5.1 lines 416-446 (G1 fan-out shape) and names the 4 impls. The 4 impls each `impl crate::executor::G1Source` (UCIL-owned trait per DEC-0008 §4):  
  (a) `pub struct KgG1Source { kg: Arc<Mutex<KnowledgeGraph>> }`. `kind() -> G1ToolKind::TreeSitter`. `execute(&query) -> G1ToolOutput` runs the symbol-references read against the KG (use `KnowledgeGraph::find_references_by_name` if present, else open a `Connection` and SELECT from the `references` table joined to `symbols` to project rows with `(file_path, start_line, end_line, usage_type)`). For each row, emit `G1FusionEntry { location: G1FusedLocation { file_path, start_line, end_line }, fields: { "usage_type": <str>, "signature": <str>, "source": "tree-sitter+kg" } }`. `usage_type` lives in `fields` (G1FusionEntry's free-form map). On KG mutex poison, return `G1ToolOutput { kind: TreeSitter, status: Errored, payload: Null, error: Some("kg mutex poisoned".to_owned()) }` — no panic, no `.unwrap()` outside `#[cfg(test)]`. Constructor `pub fn new(kg: Arc<Mutex<KnowledgeGraph>>) -> Self`.  
  (b) `pub struct SerenaG1Source { hover_client: Arc<dyn SerenaHoverClient + Send + Sync> }`. `kind() -> G1ToolKind::Serena`. `execute(&query) -> G1ToolOutput` calls `self.hover_client.hover(&query.symbol, &query.file_path, query.line, query.column).await`. On `Ok(HoverDoc { signature, doc, source })`, emit ONE `G1FusionEntry` keyed at `(query.file_path, query.line, query.line)` with fields `{ "usage_type": "definition", "signature": <hover.signature>, "hover_doc": <hover.doc>, "source": "serena" }` — Serena returns hover for the symbol's definition, which surfaces as a `definition` usage rather than call/import/type-annotation. (The handler's `usage_type` grouping bucket for `definition` collapses to `call` per master-plan §3.2 row 3 OR is included as a fourth `definition` bucket when present — F05 SHALL include `definition` as a top-level key in `_meta.references_by_usage` alongside `call`, `import`, `type-annotation`. Document this in the type's rustdoc.) On `Err(HoverFetchError::*)`, return `G1ToolOutput { kind: Serena, status: Errored | TimedOut, ..., error: Some(<format>) }`. Constructor `pub fn new(hover_client: Arc<dyn SerenaHoverClient + Send + Sync>) -> Self`.  
  (c) `pub struct AstGrepG1Source { /* placeholder for the future MCP-channel handle */ }`. `kind() -> G1ToolKind::AstGrep`. `execute(&_query) -> G1ToolOutput` ALWAYS returns `G1ToolOutput { kind: AstGrep, status: G1ToolStatus::Unavailable, elapsed_ms: 0, payload: Value::Null, error: Some("ast-grep MCP-channel wiring deferred to a follow-up WO; production-shape stub per WO-0049 scope_out".to_owned()) }`. Constructor `pub fn new() -> Self` (or `Default::default()`); document the deferral in the type's rustdoc with explicit forward-WO trigger language. The `Unavailable` status is silently dropped by `fuse_g1` (per WO-0048 partial-results semantics) — the fused output remains correct.  
  (d) `pub struct DiagnosticsG1Source { /* placeholder for the future LSP-bridge handle */ }`. `kind() -> G1ToolKind::Diagnostics`. `execute(&_query) -> G1ToolOutput` ALWAYS returns `G1ToolStatus::Unavailable` with the same deferral-shape error message naming `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics::bridge`. Constructor and rustdoc same shape as (c). NO new dependency on `ucil-lsp-diagnostics` is added here — the production wiring will require a `Cargo.toml` change in the follow-up WO.

Add `pub struct G1SourceFactory` to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g1_sources.rs`. Holds the dependencies (`Arc<Mutex<KnowledgeGraph>>` + `Arc<dyn SerenaHoverClient + Send + Sync>`) and provides `pub fn build(&self) -> Vec<Box<dyn G1Source + Send + Sync + 'static>>` returning a freshly-constructed vec of all 4 impls in `[TreeSitter, Serena, AstGrep, Diagnostics]` order (matches `G1ToolKind` source-authority list). The factory is `Clone` (cheap — just clones two Arcs) so `McpServer::Clone` keeps working. Constructor: `pub fn new(kg: Arc<Mutex<KnowledgeGraph>>, hover_client: Arc<dyn SerenaHoverClient + Send + Sync>) -> Self`.

Extend `pub struct McpServer` (in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs`) with a NEW field `pub g1_sources: Option<Arc<G1SourceFactory>>`. Add a builder method `pub fn with_g1_sources(mut self, factory: Arc<G1SourceFactory>) -> Self { self.g1_sources = Some(factory); self }` so existing constructors remain backward-compatible (the field defaults to `None`). The existing `McpServer::new()` and `McpServer::with_knowledge_graph(kg)` constructors MUST stay byte-identical in behavior — the `g1_sources` field defaults to `None`, and routing falls through to the stub path when the factory is absent. This preserves WO-0010 acceptance selector `server::test_all_22_tools_registered` and every existing P1-W4 / P1-W5 acceptance test unchanged.

Add `mod g1_sources;` and `pub use g1_sources::{G1SourceFactory, KgG1Source, SerenaG1Source, AstGrepG1Source, DiagnosticsG1Source};` to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs`. Maintain the existing alphabetical-within-block ordering. The lib.rs preamble paragraph for WO-0048 (the F02 sentence) gets a NEW trailing sentence appended: 'WO-0049 for `P2-W7-F05` lights up [`server::McpServer::with_g1_sources`] and the `find_references` MCP tool (master-plan §3.2 row 3): the handler fans out via [`executor::execute_g1`], fuses via [`executor::fuse_g1`], and groups the result by `usage_type` (`call` / `import` / `type-annotation` / `definition`). Production-shape `G1Source` impls land in [`g1_sources`]: [`g1_sources::KgG1Source`] (tree-sitter + KG) and [`g1_sources::SerenaG1Source`] (Serena hover) are productive today; [`g1_sources::AstGrepG1Source`] and [`g1_sources::DiagnosticsG1Source`] are production-shape stubs that report `G1ToolStatus::Unavailable` until their subprocess-channel wiring lands in a follow-up WO.' Same deferral-signpost discipline as WO-0045 lessons line 218 / WO-0047 lessons line 298 / WO-0048 scope_in line 23.

Register a `find_references` route in `handle_tools_call` (currently at `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs:514-571`). Routing rule: when `name == "find_references" && self.g1_sources.is_some()`, dispatch to `Self::handle_find_references(id, params, sources)`. When `g1_sources` is absent, fall through to the existing stub path (`_meta.not_yet_implemented: true`) so `McpServer::new()` (the Phase-1 default) preserves WO-0010 invariant. The route is INSERTED between the `search_code` and `understand_code` clauses to keep the handler list grouped by master-plan §3.2 row order.

Implement `fn handle_find_references(id: &Value, params: &Value, factory: &Arc<G1SourceFactory>) -> Value` on `McpServer`. The handler:  
  (a) Parse `params.arguments.name` (REQUIRED, string) and `params.arguments.file_path` (OPTIONAL, string). On missing/non-string `name`, return JSON-RPC error -32602 with message `"find_references: arguments.name is required and must be a string"` (matches the existing `find_definition` error-shape at server.rs:613-616).  
  (b) Construct `let q = G1Query { symbol: name, file_path: PathBuf::from(file_path.unwrap_or_default()), line: 1, column: 1 };`. Master-plan §5.1 line 423 names symbol/file/line/column as the query shape; line/column default to 1 when the caller did not supply a position (find_references is symbol-name-driven, not position-driven).  
  (c) Build sources via `let sources = factory.build();` (4 boxed impls).  
  (d) Call `let raw = execute_g1(q, sources, G1_MASTER_DEADLINE).await; let fused = fuse_g1(&raw);`.  
  (e) Group `fused.entries` by `fields["usage_type"]`. Use a `BTreeMap<String, Vec<Value>>` keyed by usage_type so the output JSON has deterministic ordering of buckets. For each fused entry: extract `usage_type` (string; default to `"call"` when absent — the historic master-plan §3.2 row 3 grouping says "call, import, type" with `call` as the default for unspecified use-sites), then push `{ "file_path": <location.file_path>, "start_line": <location.start_line>, "end_line": <location.end_line>, "signature": <fields.signature or null>, "contributing_sources": <serialized G1ToolKind list> }` into that bucket.  
  (f) Emit MCP envelope: `{ jsonrpc: "2.0", id, result: { _meta: { tool: "find_references", source: "g1-fused", found: <bool — true iff any bucket non-empty>, total_references: <count>, references_by_usage: <BTreeMap-serialized>, master_timed_out: <bool> }, content: [{type: "text", text: <human-readable summary>}], isError: false } }`. The not-found shape returns `isError: false` with `_meta.found == false` (matches `find_definition`'s contract per server.rs:594-597).

Add per-source children tracing spans in `run_g1_source` (`crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs:988`). Annotate the function with `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.group.structural.source", level = "debug", skip(source, query), fields(kind = ?source.kind()))]`. The parent `ucil.group.structural` span on `execute_g1` already exists at `executor.rs:1105-1110` (per WO-0047). Adding the per-source child closes the WO-0047 lessons line 296 deferral. The `kind = ?source.kind()` field uses `?` (Debug formatter) so the span field surfaces as `kind=TreeSitter` / `kind=Serena` / `kind=AstGrep` / `kind=Diagnostics` per master-plan §15.2 span-naming `ucil.<layer>.<op>` discipline.

Refactor `executor.rs:1056-1058`'s `.expect(...)` to a structurally-enforced `let-else` per WO-0047 lessons line 308 + `.claude/rules/rust-style.md`. The current code is `slots.into_iter().map(|r| r.expect("join_all_g1: every slot must be filled before returning")).collect()`. Replacement: rewrite as a `for r in slots { let Some(v) = r else { unreachable!("join_all_g1: every slot must be filled before returning"); }; out.push(v); }` style OR collect via `slots.into_iter().flatten().collect::<Vec<_>>()` with a `debug_assert_eq!(out.len(), len, "join_all_g1 invariant")`. Either replacement removes the `.expect(...)` outside `#[cfg(test)]` literally; the structural invariant (every slot is filled because the loop only exits when `!any_pending`) is preserved. Bake a one-line rustdoc comment naming the invariant.

Add the frozen acceptance test `pub async fn test_find_references_tool()` annotated `#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 4)]` at MODULE ROOT of `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests {}`) per DEC-0007. The selector matches `server::test_find_references_tool` (per `feature-list.json:P2-W7-F05`). Test setup:  
  (a) Build a TestG1SourceFactory that returns 4 local `TestG1Source` impls (per DEC-0008 §4 — `G1Source` is UCIL-owned, NOT a critical-dep mock per WO-0047 lessons line 313 + WO-0048 lessons line 363). Each TestG1Source returns a `G1ToolOutput { status: Available, payload: <serde_json::to_value(Vec<G1FusionEntry>) }` where the payload entries cover every usage_type:  
    - TreeSitter source: 3 entries — `(util.rs, 10, 10, usage_type="call")`, `(util.rs, 20, 20, usage_type="call")`, `(other.rs, 5, 5, usage_type="import")`.  
    - Serena source: 1 entry — `(util.rs, 10, 10, usage_type="call", signature="fn foo() -> i32")` (corroborates TreeSitter's first call-site with extra signature data).  
    - AstGrep source: 1 entry — `(types.rs, 30, 30, usage_type="type-annotation")`.  
    - Diagnostics source: returns `G1ToolStatus::Unavailable` (silently dropped by fuse_g1).  
  (b) Construct the McpServer via `McpServer::new().with_g1_sources(factory)`. NOTE the test does NOT need a real `Arc<Mutex<KnowledgeGraph>>` — the McpServer's `g1_sources` slot drives the test, and `kg` stays `None`.  
  (c) Call `server.handle_line(&request_json)` with a `tools/call` for `find_references` with `arguments.name = "foo"`.  
  (d) **Sub-assertion 1** — JSON-RPC envelope is well-formed: `jsonrpc == "2.0"`, `id` echoes the request, no `error` field, `result.isError == false`.  
  (e) **Sub-assertion 2** — `result._meta.tool == "find_references"`, `result._meta.source == "g1-fused"`, `result._meta.found == true`, `result._meta.master_timed_out == false`.  
  (f) **Sub-assertion 3 — usage_type bucketing**: `result._meta.references_by_usage` is a JSON object with keys `call`, `import`, `type-annotation` (BTreeMap-sorted). The `call` bucket has 2 entries (TreeSitter+Serena fused at `(util.rs, 10, 10)` PLUS the bare-TreeSitter `(util.rs, 20, 20)`). The `import` bucket has 1 entry at `(other.rs, 5, 5)`. The `type-annotation` bucket has 1 entry at `(types.rs, 30, 30)`. Each bucket is asserted by `assert_eq!` with operator-actionable panic messages quoting the actual JSON content on failure.  
  (g) **Sub-assertion 4 — fused signature wins**: the `(util.rs, 10, 10)` entry in the `call` bucket has `signature == "fn foo() -> i32"` (Serena's value, fused into the TreeSitter+Serena group by `fuse_g1`). This proves the handler is calling `fuse_g1` not just routing TreeSitter's raw output. Cite `fuse_g1` (per WO-0048) as the dependency.  
  (h) **Sub-assertion 5 — total_references**: `result._meta.total_references == 4` (the count of fused entries, NOT the count of pre-fusion source entries; TreeSitter+Serena merged at one location reduces the raw-5 entries to fused-4).

Add `pub async fn test_find_references_tool_unknown_symbol()` and `pub async fn test_find_references_tool_missing_name_param()` adjacent module-root tests covering the negative paths (mirroring `test_find_definition_tool_unknown_symbol` at server.rs:2243 and `test_find_definition_tool_missing_name_param` at server.rs:2282). The unknown-symbol test factory returns 4 sources that all yield empty `Vec<G1FusionEntry>` payloads → `_meta.found == false`, `result.isError == false`, no JSON-RPC error envelope. The missing-name-param test omits `arguments.name` → JSON-RPC `error.code == -32602` and `error.message` mentions `name`.

Add `scripts/verify/P2-W7-F05.sh` per the standing convention. Script must (a) run `cargo test -p ucil-daemon server::test_find_references_tool -- --nocapture`, (b) tee output through the WO-0042 alternation regex `grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. .* 0 failed|[0-9]+ tests? passed'`, (c) emit `[FAIL] P2-W7-F05: <reason>` on non-zero exit with a hint pointing at the assertion line. Also include a smoke-call against the negative-path tests: `cargo test -p ucil-daemon server::test_find_references_tool_unknown_symbol server::test_find_references_tool_missing_name_param -- --nocapture`. Make executable: `chmod +x scripts/verify/P2-W7-F05.sh`.

**Anti-cascade for `#![deny(warnings)]`**: per-mutation patches MUST use the runtime-only variant pattern from WO-0046 lessons line 245 — `let _ = arg;` + early `return Default::default()` — when the literal sed produces a compile-failure cascade. Document BOTH the literal sed AND the runtime-only variant for every pre-baked mutation in the `acceptance` block below. Standing protocol now in its 4th consecutive WO (WO-0046 / 0047 / 0048 / 0049).

**Compose with `execute_g1` and `fuse_g1` WITHOUT modifying their bodies.** WO-0047 froze `execute_g1`'s signature; WO-0048 froze `fuse_g1`'s signature. F05 ONLY (a) adds a `#[tracing::instrument]` attribute on the private `run_g1_source` helper (per scope_in step 7) and (b) refactors the `.expect(...)` line at executor.rs:1057 (per scope_in step 8). Neither edit changes the public surface. The 9 WO-0047 + 6 WO-0048 public symbols (`G1Source`, `G1Query`, `G1ToolKind`, `G1ToolStatus`, `G1ToolOutput`, `G1Outcome`, `execute_g1`, `G1_MASTER_DEADLINE`, `G1_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE`, `fuse_g1`, `G1Conflict`, `G1FusedEntry`, `G1FusedLocation`, `G1FusedOutcome`, `G1FusionEntry`) all stay byte-identical in their definitions.

**No new dependency on `ucil-lsp-diagnostics`.** The `DiagnosticsG1Source` impl is a production-shape stub returning `G1ToolStatus::Unavailable` — wiring the real LSP-diagnostics bridge requires a `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml` change which is OUT OF SCOPE per scope_out below. Same posture for the `AstGrepG1Source` impl — the manifest landed in WO-0044 but the runtime MCP-channel wiring (spawning `npx -y @notprolands/ast-grep-mcp@1.1.1` and exchanging JSON-RPC) is OUT OF SCOPE here. Both deferrals are documented in the type rustdocs with forward-WO trigger language.

Optional but recommended rustdoc on `handle_find_references`: include a brief `# Examples` block showing a 2-source fusion case (TreeSitter at one usage_type + Serena fusing in the same location's signature → bucketed under `call`). Backticks every uppercase identifier per WO-0043 lessons line 128 — pre-flight grep `rg -nE '^\s*///.*\b[A-Z][A-Z_0-9]+\b' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs crates/ucil-daemon/src/g1_sources.rs` returns zero hits OR every match is inside backticks (per AC02).
scope_out: **No real ast-grep MCP-channel wiring.** `AstGrepG1Source` ships as a production-shape stub returning `G1ToolStatus::Unavailable`. The real wiring (spawn `npx -y @notprolands/ast-grep-mcp@1.1.1` per WO-0044's manifest, exchange `tools/call` with `find_references` arguments, decode response into `G1FusionEntry`) is deferred to a follow-up WO that touches the `PluginManager` channel surface. Trigger: when the MCP-client trait surface lands.

**No real LSP-diagnostics bridge wiring.** `DiagnosticsG1Source` ships as a production-shape stub returning `G1ToolStatus::Unavailable`. The real wiring (instantiate `ucil_lsp_diagnostics::bridge::DiagnosticsBridge`, query references via call-hierarchy + reference-by-position, decode into `G1FusionEntry`) is deferred to a follow-up WO that adds the `ucil-lsp-diagnostics` workspace dependency. Trigger: when the diagnostics bridge surfaces a stable references API.

**No `Cargo.toml` modifications.** Every dep needed by F05 (`tokio`, `tracing`, `serde_json`, `async-trait`, `ucil-treesitter`, `ucil-core`, `tempfile`) is already in `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml`. No new workspace deps. Verifier confirms via `git diff --name-only main...HEAD -- '*.toml'` returning empty.

**No changes to existing MCP-tool handlers.** `handle_find_definition` / `handle_get_conventions` / `handle_search_code` / `handle_understand_code` stay byte-identical. F05 ADDS one new route (`find_references`) and one new method (`handle_find_references`) — does NOT modify existing ones. WO-0010 acceptance selector `server::test_all_22_tools_registered` MUST stay green.

**No changes to `execute_g1` / `fuse_g1` bodies.** Per scope_in step 13. The WO-0047 + WO-0048 public surface stays byte-identical; F05 ONLY adds `#[tracing::instrument]` on the private `run_g1_source` and refactors the `.expect(...)` line at executor.rs:1057.

**No changes to `crates/ucil-treesitter/**`, `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/**`, `crates/ucil-core/**`, `crates/ucil-cli/**`, `crates/ucil-embeddings/**`, `crates/ucil-agents/**`, `adapters/**`, `ml/**`, `plugins/**`, `tests/fixtures/**`, `tests/integration/**`, `tests/scenarios/**`, `tests/benchmarks/**`** — F05 is contained to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/{server.rs, executor.rs, g1_sources.rs (NEW), lib.rs}` plus one new verify script.
**No SCIP integration.** P2-W7-F08 lands SCIP P1; F05 does NOT touch the SCIP path. The 4-variant `G1ToolKind` (frozen by WO-0047) is the source-set for F05. SCIP is a fifth variant landed in F08.

**No agent-narrative synthesis.** Master-plan §5.1 lines 437-439 mention an Interpreter agent producing `agent_narrative`; that's a Phase-3 / agent-system feature outside F05's scope. The handler emits raw fused JSON; agent-narrative synthesis is layered on later.

**No changes to existing tests.** `test_find_definition_tool` / `test_get_conventions_tool` / `test_search_code_fused` / `test_understand_code_*` / `test_all_22_tools_registered` / WO-0047's `test_g1_parallel_execution` / WO-0048's `test_g1_result_fusion` / WO-0046's `test_plugin_lifecycle` / Phase-1 e2e tests (`e2e_mcp_stdio`, `e2e_mcp_with_kg`) all stay byte-identical. F05 ADDS three new tests at module root of server.rs.

**No new ADR.** DEC-0005 (module-coherence commits up to ~200 LOC), DEC-0007 (frozen-selector module-root placement), DEC-0008 (UCIL-internal trait boundaries — `G1Source` is the seam), DEC-0011 (crate-scoped test-path guard) cover the WO. If a NEW pattern surfaces (e.g. `Arc<dyn G1Source>` lifetime issues with the `Vec<Box<...>>` consume-by-move signature, surprising serde interactions on `G1FusionEntry` deserialization, factory pattern leakage), STOP and write an ADR before working around.

**No `cargo-mutants` per-WO gate.** DEC-0007 removed it; pre-baked function-body mutations in `acceptance` are the authoritative anti-laziness layer (carry from WO-0042 / 0043 / 0044 / 0045 / 0046 / 0047 / 0048, now 7 consecutive).
acceptance: AC01 — `cargo build -p ucil-daemon` exits 0.

AC02 — `cargo clippy -p ucil-daemon --all-targets -- -D warnings` exits 0. Pre-flight grep `rg -nE '^\s*///.*\b[A-Z][A-Z_0-9]+\b' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs crates/ucil-daemon/src/g1_sources.rs crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` returns ZERO new hits relative to `git show main:<file> | rg -cE '^\s*///.*\b[A-Z][A-Z_0-9]+\b'` (per WO-0043 lessons line 128 `clippy::doc_markdown` discipline).
AC03 — `cargo test -p ucil-daemon server::test_find_references_tool -- --nocapture` exits 0.

AC04 — Test selector `server::test_find_references_tool` lives at MODULE ROOT of `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests {}`) per DEC-0007. Verifier checks via `grep -nE '^pub async fn test_find_references_tool' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` returning a non-`mod tests {}`-nested line.

AC05 — Sub-assertion 1 (envelope well-formed): `jsonrpc == "2.0"`, `id` echoes the request, `result.isError == false`, no `error` field. Operator-readable panic message MUST quote the response JSON on failure.
AC06 — Sub-assertion 2 (meta fields): `result._meta.tool == "find_references"`, `result._meta.source == "g1-fused"`, `result._meta.found == true`, `result._meta.master_timed_out == false`.

AC07 — Sub-assertion 3 (usage_type bucketing): `result._meta.references_by_usage` is a JSON object with EXACTLY the 3 keys `call`, `import`, `type-annotation` (BTreeMap-sorted output). The `call` bucket has 2 entries; `import` has 1; `type-annotation` has 1. Total entries = 4. Each assertion uses `assert_eq!` with operator-readable panic messages quoting the actual bucket content.

AC08 — Sub-assertion 4 (fused signature wins): the `(util.rs, 10, 10)` entry in the `call` bucket has `signature == "fn foo() -> i32"` (Serena's value fused with TreeSitter's row at the same location). Proves the handler is calling `fuse_g1` rather than routing raw source output.

AC09 — Sub-assertion 5 (total_references): `result._meta.total_references == 4`. Counts FUSED entries (post-merge), not pre-fusion source entries (which would total 5: TreeSitter's 3 + Serena's 1 + AstGrep's 1). Proves the count comes from `G1FusedOutcome.entries.len()`, not `G1Outcome.results.iter().sum(...)`.
